content.content:
1	# DroneAid ??? Concept & Function List
2	
3	- **Date:** 2026-05-19
4	- **Course:** CSC291, KMUTT
5	- **Team:** Aok (Lead), Belle, Bew, Poom, Tawan
6	- **Status:** Approved
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. Problem statement
11	
12	In active conflict zones, ground supply lines for civilians are dangerous, slow, and unreliable. Relief organizations need to move small, time-critical packages (food kits, water, medical supplies, blankets) over short distances without exposing human couriers to combat or destroyed infrastructure.
13	
14	Drones provide a fast, low-risk delivery mechanism, but coordinating them at scale requires:
15	
16	- A way for affected civilians to **request** specific supplies.
17	- A way for relief coordinators to **vet and dispatch** drones based on payload, range, and current weather.
18	- A way for everyone to **track delivery in real time**, with visibility into failure conditions (storms, low battery, mechanical fault).
19	
20	**DroneAid** is a mobile application that simulates this end-to-end system. The drone fleet is fully simulated ??? no real hardware ??? but the data model, control loop, and failure modes mirror what a real deployment would face.
21	
22	## 2. Vision
23	
24	> A civilian in an impacted area opens DroneAid, picks the relief supplies they need from a catalog, marks a delivery pin on the map, and submits. A coordinator reviews, assigns a drone, and the user watches the drone fly in on a live map. Battery, ETA, and weather are visible the whole time. If a storm aborts the flight, both sides are notified and a new drone is dispatched.
25	
26	## 3. Goals
27	
28	1. **End-to-end delivery flow** working under multi-user, real-time conditions.
29	2. **Realistic simulation** with weather, battery, and mechanical failure modes that affect outcomes.
30	3. **Single mobile codebase** serving both end-user and admin roles.
31	4. **Auditable Claude Code usage** ??? every team member's session logs are committed to the repo (per professor's requirement).
32	5. **Demonstrable on emulator** for class presentation + screencast.
33	
34	## 4. Non-goals (scope cut for 2-week timeline)
35	
36	- Real weather API integration (admin sets weather manually).
37	- Real SMS/OTP authentication (synthetic email under the hood).
38	- Government identity verification (format + checksum only, no API call).
39	- Payment, scoring, or rating system.
40	- Admin analytics dashboard (deferred).
41	- iOS push notifications (Android FCM only).
42	- Multi-tenancy or multi-org support.
43	
44	## 5. Personas (summary ??? full versions in `03-personas.docx`)
45	
46	| Persona | Role |
47	|---|---|
48	| **Mali Suwan** (37, refugee mother of two) | Primary user ??? requests supplies for family in a shelter |
49	| **Naree Charoen** (29, relief coordinator) | Admin user ??? vets requests, dispatches drones, monitors fleet |
50	
51	## 6. Functional list
52	
53	### 6.1 Authentication & profile
54	
55	- F-AUTH-01 Register with Thai national ID (13 digits + checksum) + password + name + phone.
56	- F-AUTH-02 Log in with national ID + password.
57	- F-AUTH-03 Log out, with FCM token cleanup.
58	- F-AUTH-04 Edit profile (name, phone, delivery address pin, preferred language).
59	- F-AUTH-05 Admin can lock/unlock a user account.
60	- F-AUTH-06 Auto-provision `users/{uid}` document with `role: "user"` on first sign-in.
61	- F-AUTH-07 Seed admin accounts via one-time script.
62	
63	### 6.2 Catalog & supply
64	
65	- F-CAT-01 Display fixed catalog of relief items (name, weight, stock, icon).
66	- F-CAT-02 Filter catalog to active + in-stock items for user picker.
67	- F-CAT-03 Admin can create new catalog item.
68	- F-CAT-04 Admin can restock an item (add to stock).
69	- F-CAT-05 Admin can deactivate an item (hides from user picker).
70	
71	### 6.3 Request lifecycle (user side)
72	
73	- F-REQ-01 Browse catalog and add items to cart with quantity.
74	- F-REQ-02 Pick delivery pin on map (defaults to profile delivery address).
75	- F-REQ-03 Submit request; backend validates stock + total weight ??? max drone payload.
76	- F-REQ-04 View own queue of requests with live status updates.
77	- F-REQ-05 Cancel an own request while still pending.
78	- F-REQ-06 View history of completed / failed / cancelled requests.
79	
80	### 6.4 Request lifecycle (admin side)
81	
82	- F-ADM-01 List all pending and active requests, filtered by status and priority.
83	- F-ADM-02 Open a request and view full user profile + request details.
84	- F-ADM-03 Approve a request (atomically decrement stock).
85	- F-ADM-04 Reject a request with a reason string.
86	- F-ADM-05 Select an eligible drone from a filtered picker (idle, payload OK, base in range).
87	- F-ADM-06 Assign the selected drone to the request, creating a flight document.
88	
89	### 6.5 Drone fleet
90	
91	- F-FLT-01 List all drones including offline + maintenance.
92	- F-FLT-02 View drone detail: status, battery, current flight, future queue.
93	- F-FLT-03 Toggle a drone into maintenance mode (removes from picker).
94	- F-FLT-04 Seed an initial fleet of 8 drones with realistic positions and IDs.
95	
96	### 6.6 Delivery tracking & simulation
97	
98	- F-SIM-01 Scheduled Cloud Function `tickFlights` runs every 60 seconds.
99	- F-SIM-02 Compute drone progress from `takeoffAt`, distance, speed, weather modifier.
100	- F-SIM-03 Drain battery over elapsed time; faster drain during storm.
101	- F-SIM-04 Roll three failure types per tick while in flight: weather (20% during storm), battery (< 15%), mechanical (1%).
102	- F-SIM-05 Transition flight states: enroute ??? delivering ??? completed ??? returning ??? idle.
103	- F-SIM-06 Client-side interpolation of drone position every frame using `lerp(origin, destination, t)`.
104	- F-SIM-07 Show live battery, ETA, and weather state on tracking page.
105	- F-SIM-08 Admin can globally set weather state to clear / wind / storm.
106	
107	### 6.7 Delivery confirmation
108	
109	- F-CFM-01 When status transitions to `delivered`, user sees Confirm page prompt.
110	- F-CFM-02 User taps Confirm to finalize; request becomes `confirmed`.
111	- F-CFM-03 Confirmed flight triggers drone return-to-base.
112	- F-CFM-04 Drone returning + arrived ??? drone status becomes `idle`.
113	
114	### 6.8 Notifications
115	
116	- F-NTF-01 Push notification via FCM on every state transition for owning user.
117	- F-NTF-02 Push notification to all admins when a new request is submitted.
118	- F-NTF-03 Push notification to admins on flight failure.
119	- F-NTF-04 In-app notifications inbox lists all events with read state.
120	- F-NTF-05 Tap notification jumps to associated request or flight.
121	
122	### 6.9 Security
123	
124	- F-SEC-01 Direct client writes to `requests`, `flights`, `drones`, `notifications` are denied by Firestore rules.
125	- F-SEC-02 All state-changing actions go through callable Cloud Functions.
126	- F-SEC-03 Callable functions enforce role server-side (`context.auth` + user doc lookup).
127	- F-SEC-04 `gitleaks` CI gate blocks accidental secret commits.
128	- F-SEC-05 Local `redact-secrets.py` strips API keys + national IDs from agent logs before commit.
129	
130	### 6.10 Auditability
131	
132	- F-AUD-01 Each team member's Claude Code session JSONL copied to `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` via `SessionEnd` hook.
133	- F-AUD-02 CI job `log-presence` blocks PRs by author X that touch source but contain no new log under their folder.
134	- F-AUD-03 Auto-generated `_index.md` lists every session by date + author + summary.
135	
136	## 7. Tech stack summary
137	
138	- **Mobile:** Flutter (Android primary, iOS stretch)
139	- **State:** Riverpod
140	- **Routing:** go_router
141	- **Map:** flutter_map + OpenStreetMap tiles
142	- **Backend:** Firebase (Auth, Firestore, Cloud Functions in TypeScript, FCM)
143	- **Scheduled jobs:** Cloud Functions `onSchedule`
144	- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions
145	- **Local dev:** Firebase Emulator Suite
146	
147	(Full rationale in design spec, ??2.)
148	
149	## 8. Acceptance criteria
150	
151	See design spec ??16. Summary:
152	
153	- Register ??? submit request ??? admin approve ??? admin assign drone ??? live tracking ??? confirm receipt ??? drone returns home ??? idle.
154	- Storm weather can abort an in-flight drone; notification reaches user + admin; admin can reassign a new drone.
155	- All Firestore writes mediated by Cloud Functions or guarded by rules.
156	- Every dev has at least one Claude Code session log under `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` for every day they committed code.
157	
158	## 9. Related documents
159	
160	- [00 ??? Team & RACI](00-team-raci.md)
161	- [02 ??? Backlog (Wideband Delphi)](02-backlog-delphi.xlsx)
162	- [03 ??? Personas](03-personas.docx)
163	- [04 ??? User Journey Map](04-journey-map.md)
164	- [05 ??? GANTT Chart](05-gantt.xlsx)
165	- [06 ??? Work Breakdown Structure](06-wbs.md)
166	- [07 ??? Software Architecture](07-software-architecture.md)
167	- [08 ??? Implementation Diagram](08-implementation-diagram.md)
168	- [Design Spec (full)](superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-drone-relief-design.md)
169

# DroneAid ??? Concept & Function List

- **Date:** 2026-05-19
- **Course:** CSC291, KMUTT
- **Team:** Aok (Lead), Belle, Bew, Poom, Tawan
- **Status:** Approved

---

## 1. Problem statement

In active conflict zones, ground supply lines for civilians are dangerous, slow, and unreliable. Relief organizations need to move small, time-critical packages (food kits, water, medical supplies, blankets) over short distances without exposing human couriers to combat or destroyed infrastructure.

Drones provide a fast, low-risk delivery mechanism, but coordinating them at scale requires:

- A way for affected civilians to **request** specific supplies.
- A way for relief coordinators to **vet and dispatch** drones based on payload, range, and current weather.
- A way for everyone to **track delivery in real time**, with visibility into failure conditions (storms, low battery, mechanical fault).

**DroneAid** is a mobile application that simulates this end-to-end system. The drone fleet is fully simulated ??? no real hardware ??? but the data model, control loop, and failure modes mirror what a real deployment would face.

## 2. Vision

> A civilian in an impacted area opens DroneAid, picks the relief supplies they need from a catalog, marks a delivery pin on the map, and submits. A coordinator reviews, assigns a drone, and the user watches the drone fly in on a live map. Battery, ETA, and weather are visible the whole time. If a storm aborts the flight, both sides are notified and a new drone is dispatched.

## 3. Goals

1. **End-to-end delivery flow** working under multi-user, real-time conditions.
2. **Realistic simulation** with weather, battery, and mechanical failure modes that affect outcomes.
3. **Single mobile codebase** serving both end-user and admin roles.
4. **Auditable Claude Code usage** ??? every team member's session logs are committed to the repo (per professor's requirement).
5. **Demonstrable on emulator** for class presentation + screencast.

## 4. Non-goals (scope cut for 2-week timeline)

- Real weather API integration (admin sets weather manually).
- Real SMS/OTP authentication (synthetic email under the hood).
- Government identity verification (format + checksum only, no API call).
- Payment, scoring, or rating system.
- Admin analytics dashboard (deferred).
- iOS push notifications (Android FCM only).
- Multi-tenancy or multi-org support.

## 5. Personas (summary ??? full versions in `03-personas.docx`)

| Persona | Role |
|---|---|
| **Mali Suwan** (37, refugee mother of two) | Primary user ??? requests supplies for family in a shelter |
| **Naree Charoen** (29, relief coordinator) | Admin user ??? vets requests, dispatches drones, monitors fleet |

## 6. Functional list

### 6.1 Authentication & profile

- F-AUTH-02 Log in with national ID + password.
- F-AUTH-03 Log out, with FCM token cleanup.

- F-AUTH-07 Seed admin accounts via one-time script.

### 6.2 Catalog & supply

- F-CAT-01 Display fixed catalog of relief items (name, weight, stock, icon).
- F-CAT-02 Filter catalog to active + in-stock items for user picker.
- F-CAT-03 Admin can create new catalog item.
- F-CAT-04 Admin can restock an item (add to stock).
- F-CAT-05 Admin can deactivate an item (hides from user picker).

### 6.3 Request lifecycle (user side)

- F-REQ-01 Browse catalog and add items to cart with quantity.
- F-REQ-02 Pick delivery pin on map (defaults to profile delivery address).
- F-REQ-03 Submit request; backend validates stock + total weight ??? max drone payload.
- F-REQ-04 View own queue of requests with live status updates.
- F-REQ-05 Cancel an own request while still pending.
- F-REQ-06 View history of completed / failed / cancelled requests.

### 6.4 Request lifecycle (admin side)

- F-ADM-01 List all pending and active requests, filtered by status and priority.
- F-ADM-02 Open a request and view full user profile + request details.
- F-ADM-03 Approve a request (atomically decrement stock).
- F-ADM-04 Reject a request with a reason string.
- F-ADM-05 Select an eligible drone from a filtered picker (idle, payload OK, base in range).
- F-ADM-06 Assign the selected drone to the request, creating a flight document.

### 6.5 Drone fleet

### 6.6 Delivery tracking & simulation

- F-SIM-01 Scheduled Cloud Function `tickFlights` runs every 60 seconds.
- F-SIM-02 Compute drone progress from `takeoffAt`, distance, speed, weather modifier.
- F-SIM-03 Drain battery over elapsed time; faster drain during storm.
- F-SIM-04 Roll three failure types per tick while in flight: weather (20% during storm), battery (< 15%), mechanical (1%).
- F-SIM-05 Transition flight states: enroute ??? delivering ??? completed ??? returning ??? idle.
- F-SIM-06 Client-side interpolation of drone position every frame using `lerp(origin, destination, t)`.
- F-SIM-07 Show live battery, ETA, and weather state on tracking page.
- F-SIM-08 Admin can globally set weather state to clear / wind / storm.

### 6.7 Delivery confirmation

- F-CFM-01 When status transitions to `delivered`, user sees Confirm page prompt.
- F-CFM-02 User taps Confirm to finalize; request becomes `confirmed`.
- F-CFM-03 Confirmed flight triggers drone return-to-base.
- F-CFM-04 Drone returning + arrived ??? drone status becomes `idle`.

### 6.8 Notifications

- F-NTF-01 Push notification via FCM on every state transition for owning user.
- F-NTF-02 Push notification to all admins when a new request is submitted.
- F-NTF-03 Push notification to admins on flight failure.
- F-NTF-04 In-app notifications inbox lists all events with read state.
- F-NTF-05 Tap notification jumps to associated request or flight.

### 6.9 Security

- F-SEC-01 Direct client writes to `requests`, `flights`, `drones`, `notifications` are denied by Firestore rules.
- F-SEC-02 All state-changing actions go through callable Cloud Functions.
- F-SEC-03 Callable functions enforce role server-side (`context.auth` + user doc lookup).
- F-SEC-04 `gitleaks` CI gate blocks accidental secret commits.
- F-SEC-05 Local `redact-secrets.py` strips API keys + national IDs from agent logs before commit.

### 6.10 Auditability

- F-AUD-01 Each team member's Claude Code session JSONL copied to `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` via `SessionEnd` hook.
- F-AUD-02 CI job `log-presence` blocks PRs by author X that touch source but contain no new log under their folder.
- F-AUD-03 Auto-generated `_index.md` lists every session by date + author + summary.

## 7. Tech stack summary

- **Mobile:** Flutter (Android primary, iOS stretch)
- **State:** Riverpod
- **Routing:** go_router
- **Map:** flutter_map + OpenStreetMap tiles
- **Backend:** Firebase (Auth, Firestore, Cloud Functions in TypeScript, FCM)
- **Scheduled jobs:** Cloud Functions `onSchedule`
- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions
- **Local dev:** Firebase Emulator Suite

(Full rationale in design spec, ??2.)

## 8. Acceptance criteria

See design spec ??16. Summary:

- Register ??? submit request ??? admin approve ??? admin assign drone ??? live tracking ??? confirm receipt ??? drone returns home ??? idle.
- Storm weather can abort an in-flight drone; notification reaches user + admin; admin can reassign a new drone.
- All Firestore writes mediated by Cloud Functions or guarded by rules.
- Every dev has at least one Claude Code session log under `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` for every day they committed code.

## 9. Related documents

- [00 ??? Team & RACI](00-team-raci.md)
- [02 ??? Backlog (Wideband Delphi)](02-backlog-delphi.xlsx)
- [03 ??? Personas](03-personas.docx)
- [04 ??? User Journey Map](04-journey-map.md)
- [05 ??? GANTT Chart](05-gantt.xlsx)
- [06 ??? Work Breakdown Structure](06-wbs.md)
- [07 ??? Software Architecture](07-software-architecture.md)
- [08 ??? Implementation Diagram](08-implementation-diagram.md)
- [Design Spec (full)](superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-drone-relief-design.md)

# DroneAid ??? User Journey Map

Two journeys, two personas. Mermaid source inline; rendered PNGs in `diagrams/`.

- **Persona 1 (User):** Mali Suwan ??? 37, refugee mother of two, displaced to a shelter, limited cellular signal, smartphone literate but cautious.
- **Persona 2 (Admin):** Naree Charoen ??? 29, relief coordinator, manages dispatch from a forward base, monitors weather + fleet.

Full personas in [03-personas.docx](03-personas.docx).

Emotion scale (Mermaid `journey` syntax): **1 = very negative ?? 3 = neutral ?? 5 = very positive**.

---

## Journey A ??? Happy path (medical supplies delivered)

### Context

Mali's youngest has a fever. She needs a medical kit (paracetamol, ORS, basic antiseptic) tonight. Weather is clear. Naree is on-shift with 6 idle drones at the warehouse 4 km away.

### Stages (annotated)

| # | Stage | Mali's emotion | Naree's emotion | Touchpoint | System event |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Mali realizes she needs supplies | 2 (worried) | ??? | Personal | ??? |
| 2 | Mali opens DroneAid, logs in with ID | 4 | ??? | App: Login | Auth |
| 3 | Mali browses catalog, picks Medical Kit ??1 | 4 | ??? | App: Request | ??? |
| 4 | Mali drops pin near shelter, submits | 4 | ??? | App: Request | `submitRequest` callable |
| 5 | Mali sees status "Pending" in Queue | 3 | ??? | App: Queue | Live stream |
| 6 | Naree sees new request alert | ??? | 4 | App: Requests + FCM push | FCM fan-out |
| 7 | Naree opens request, sees Mali's profile + items | ??? | 4 | App: Request Manage | ??? |
| 8 | Naree taps Approve; stock decrements | ??? | 4 | App: Request Manage | `approveRequest` |
| 9 | Naree picks DRN-003 from eligible drone list | ??? | 4 | App: Drone picker | ??? |
| 10 | Naree taps Assign; flight created, drone takes off | ??? | 5 | App: Drone picker | `assignDrone` ??? flight |
| 11 | Mali gets push "Drone dispatched, ETA 18 min" | 5 | ??? | FCM + Notifications | `onFlightWritten` |
| 12 | Mali watches drone move across map | 4 | ??? | App: Tracking | Client interpolation |
| 13 | Mali sees battery 78%, ETA 6 min | 4 | ??? | App: Tracking | ??? |
| 14 | Drone arrives, status changes to Delivering | 4 | ??? | App: Tracking | `tickFlights` transition |
| 15 | Mali hears drone outside, retrieves supplies | 5 | ??? | Physical | ??? |
| 16 | Mali taps Confirm in app | 5 | ??? | App: Confirm | `confirmDelivery` |
| 17 | Drone returns, lands at base, drone returns to idle | ??? | 5 | App: Control | `tickFlights` returning ??? idle |
| 18 | Request appears in History | 5 | ??? | App: History | ??? |

### Mermaid source

```mermaid
journey
    title Journey A ??? Happy path: Mali receives medical kit
    section Realize need
      Family member sick: 2: Mali
    section Open app & submit
      Log in with national ID: 4: Mali
      Pick medical kit from catalog: 4: Mali
      Drop pin near shelter: 4: Mali
      Submit request: 4: Mali
    section Wait for approval
      See "Pending" in Queue: 3: Mali
      New request push to admins: 4: Naree
    section Admin dispatch
      Review request + user profile: 4: Naree
      Approve request: 4: Naree
      Pick eligible drone: 4: Naree
      Assign drone, flight created: 5: Naree
    section Live tracking
      Get "Drone dispatched" push: 5: Mali
      Watch drone on map: 4: Mali
      See battery and ETA update: 4: Mali
    section Delivery
      Drone enters Delivering state: 4: Mali
      Retrieve supplies physically: 5: Mali
      Tap Confirm: 5: Mali
    section Wrap up
      Drone returns to base idle: 5: Naree
      Request shown in History: 5: Mali
```

### Pain points + opportunities (Journey A)

| Pain | Mitigation in product |
|---|---|
| Wait between submit and approval feels uncertain | Live status streaming; push when admin opens the request |
| Tracking the dot on a small map may strain low-end phones | Cap fleet markers visible to current flight + cache tiles |
| Confirm step easy to miss | Persistent banner + push; later: 24h auto-confirm reminder |

---

## Journey B ??? Failure + reassign (storm aborts first drone)

### Context

Mali submits a food + water request. Weather state is `storm` (Naree set it earlier when wind picked up). She expects delays but doesn't realize storms can fully abort flights.

### Stages

| # | Stage | Mali's emotion | Naree's emotion | Touchpoint | System event |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Mali submits food kit + water ??2 | 4 | ??? | App: Request | `submitRequest` |
| 2 | Naree sees alert; weather is storm | ??? | 3 | App + FCM | ??? |
| 3 | Naree approves anyway (high priority) | ??? | 3 | App: Request Manage | `approveRequest` |
| 4 | Naree picks DRN-005 (idle, in range) | ??? | 3 | App | `assignDrone` |
| 5 | Drone takes off at reduced speed (weather mod 0.5) | 4 | 3 | App: Tracking + Control | ??? |
| 6 | Mali watches drone struggle with slow ETA | 3 | ??? | App: Tracking | ??? |
| 7 | `tickFlights` rolls 20% storm dice ??? ABORT | ??? | 2 | Backend | Flight status `aborted` |
| 8 | Mali gets push "Flight aborted, drone returning" | 2 | ??? | FCM | `onFlightWritten` |
| 9 | Naree gets push "DRN-005 aborted, reassign needed" | ??? | 2 | FCM | ??? |
| 10 | Naree opens request, taps Reassign | ??? | 3 | App: Request Manage | ??? |
| 11 | Naree filters fleet: only DRN-002 + DRN-007 OK | ??? | 3 | App | ??? |
| 12 | Naree sets weather to `wind` (storm passing) | ??? | 4 | App: Weather panel | `setWeather` |
| 13 | Naree assigns DRN-002 | ??? | 4 | App | `assignDrone` (2nd flight) |
| 14 | Mali sees new drone dispatched, restored hope | 4 | ??? | FCM + Tracking | ??? |
| 15 | Drone arrives without incident | 5 | ??? | App: Tracking | ??? |
| 16 | Mali confirms, history shows both flights | 5 | ??? | App: History | ??? |

### Mermaid source

```mermaid
journey
    title Journey B ??? Failure + reassign: storm aborts first drone
    section Submit during bad weather
      Submit food + water request: 4: Mali
      Admin alert during storm: 3: Naree
    section First dispatch
      Approve high-priority request: 3: Naree
      Assign DRN-005: 3: Naree
      Drone takes off slowly: 4: Mali
    section Mid-flight failure
      Watch slow ETA: 3: Mali
      Storm dice roll triggers abort: 2: Naree
      Push "Flight aborted": 2: Mali
      Push "Reassign needed": 2: Naree
    section Recovery
      Open request, choose Reassign: 3: Naree
      Set weather to wind: 4: Naree
      Assign DRN-002: 4: Naree
      User sees new drone dispatched: 4: Mali
    section Second-attempt delivery
      Drone arrives without incident: 5: Mali
      Confirm receipt: 5: Mali
      History shows both flight attempts: 5: Mali
```

### Pain points + opportunities (Journey B)

| Pain | Mitigation in product |
|---|---|
| User unaware that storm can fully ground drones | Show weather badge + warning banner on Tracking; explain in onboarding |
| Reassign step requires admin to open Request Manage again | Surface Reassign action directly from failure push notification |
| Two flights on one request can confuse users | History entry groups attempts under the same `requestId` |

---

## How to render the PNGs

From the repo root:

```bash
npx --yes @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli -i docs/04-journey-map.md -o docs/diagrams/04-journey-map.png
```

Mermaid CLI extracts every fenced ```mermaid block in the input markdown and emits one PNG per block. Output: `04-journey-map-1.png` (Journey A) and `04-journey-map-2.png` (Journey B).

# DroneAid ??? Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)

Deliverable-oriented decomposition of all project work into ~5 levels. Owner shown in brackets.

## Mermaid mindmap

```mermaid
mindmap
  root((DroneAid))
    1. Planning & Documentation
      1.1 Concept & function list
      1.2 Personas
      1.3 User journey map
      1.4 Backlog (Wideband Delphi)
      1.5 GANTT chart
      1.6 WBS
      1.7 Software architecture
      1.8 Implementation diagram
      1.9 Design spec
      1.10 Team RACI
    2. Backend [Aok]
      2.1 Firebase project setup
      2.2 Firestore rules + indexes
      2.3 Data models + repository contracts
      2.4 Auth trigger onUserCreated
      2.5 Callables
        submitRequest
        approveRequest
        rejectRequest
        assignDrone
        confirmDelivery
        cancelRequest
        setWeather

        Catalog
        Drones
        Admins

    3. Identity + Shared UI [Belle]
      3.1 Flutter app scaffold

      3.5 Profile page

    4. Request Domain [Bew]

    5. Tracking + Maps [Poom]

      9.1 Demo seed dataset
      9.2 Screenshots
      9.3 Screencast
      9.4 README
      9.5 Presentation slides
      9.6 Final polish
```

(haversine, lerp)"] 131 utime["time_fmt"] 132 end 133 end 134 135 router --> authgate 136 authgate --> userTree 137 authgate --> adminTree 138 userTree --> shared 139 adminTree --> shared 140 userTree --> providers 141 adminTree --> providers 142 providers --> repos 143 repos -.->|Firebase SDK| ext((Firebase)) 144 utrack --> ugeo 145 aweather --> rwx 146 urequest --> rcat 147 armgr --> rreq 148 ``` 149 150 ### Layer responsibilities 151 152 | Layer | Owns | 153 |---|---| 154 | **Pages** (`features/user/*`, `features/admin/*`) | UI only. Reads state from providers, calls repo methods through providers. | 155 | **Providers** (`providers/*`) | Riverpod state + side effects. Streams from repos, debounces UI, holds form state. | 156 | **Repositories** (`data/repositories/*`) | Single touchpoint to Firebase SDK. Returns models, never Firestore types. Mockable for tests. | 157 | **Models** (`data/models/*`) | Pure Dart classes with `fromMap`/`toMap`. No SDK imports. | 158 | **Shared widgets** (`widgets/*`) | Stateless reusables. Owned by Belle, consumed everywhere. | 159 | **Utils** (`utils/*`) | Pure functions: geo math, ID validation, time formatting. | 160 161 ### Why this layering 162 163 - Pages never import `cloud_firestore` directly ??? swapping to a different backend or mocking in tests is local change. 164 - Providers own caching + lifecycle ??? screens don't redo work on rebuild. 165 - Repos return models with named fields ??? no `data['status']` string scattering. 166 167 --- 168 169 ## 4. Cloud Functions ??? internal components 170 171 ```mermaid 172 flowchart TB 173 subgraph fnpkg["functions/"] 174 direction TB 175 idx["index.ts
(barrel exports)"] 176 177 subgraph callable["callable/"] 178 c1["submitRequest"] 179 c2["approveRequest"] 180 c3["rejectRequest"] 181 c4["assignDrone"] 182 c5["confirmDelivery"] 183 c6["cancelRequest"] 184 c7["setWeather"] 185 c8["restockItem"] 186 c9["toggleDroneMaintenance"] 187 end 188 189 subgraph scheduled["scheduled/"] 190 s1["tickFlights
(every 60s)"] 191 end 192 193 subgraph triggers["triggers/"] 194 t1["onUserCreated"] 195 t2["onFlightWritten
(state-transition FCM)"] 196 end 197 198 subgraph lib["lib/"] 199 l1["sim/
(physics, dice)"] 200 l2["geo.ts
(haversine)"] 201 l3["weather.ts
(modifier table)"] 202 l4["fcm.ts
(send wrapper)"] 203 l5["id.ts
(checksum)"] 204 l6["roles.ts
(requireAdmin, requireUser)"] 205 end 206 207 subgraph seed["seed/"] 208 d1["seedCatalog"] 209 d2["seedDrones"] 210 d3["seedAdmins"] 211 end 212 end 213 214 idx --> callable 215 idx --> scheduled 216 idx --> triggers 217 callable --> lib 218 scheduled --> lib 219 triggers --> lib 220 callable -. "auth check" .-> l6 221 s1 --> l1 222 s1 --> l3 223 s1 --> l2 224 t2 --> l4 225 ``` 226 227 ### Function responsibilities 228 229 | Component | Purpose | 230 |---|---| 231 | `callable/*` | One `https.onCall` per business action. Validate input + role; perform Firestore transaction; return result. | 232 | `scheduled/tickFlights` | Cron every 60s. Pulls active flights, advances state, rolls failures, writes transitions. | 233 | `triggers/onUserCreated` | Auth trigger. Creates `users/{uid}` with role=user. | 234 | `triggers/onFlightWritten` | Firestore trigger. On flight status change, fans out FCM to user + admins. | 235 | `lib/sim` | Pure sim functions, testable in isolation. | 236 | `lib/roles` | `requireAdmin(context)`, `requireUser(context)` helpers. | 237 | `seed/*` | Idempotent scripts to populate Firestore for demo. | 238 239 --- 240 241 ## 5. Cross-cutting concerns 242 243 | Concern | How it's handled | 244 |---|---| 245 | **Auth** | Firebase Auth on the wire; `context.auth.uid` + `users/{uid}.role` for authorization inside callables. | 246 | **Realtime** | Firestore listeners for queue, drone, flight, weather. Battery + position derived client-side from stable flight plan doc. | 247 | **Concurrency** | Stock decrement, drone assignment use Firestore transactions inside callables. | 248 | **Security rules** | DENY direct writes to mutable collections; only Functions write. | 249 | **Failure handling** | All callable returns include error codes via `HttpsError`; client maps codes to localized messages. | 250 | **Testing** | Widget tests with mock repos; Function tests against emulator; rules tests via `@firebase/rules-unit-testing`. | 251 | **Observability** | Cloud Functions logs; Firestore audit collection deferred (non-goal v1). | 252 253 --- 254 255 ## 6. Render the PNG 256 257 ```bash 258 npx --yes @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli -i docs/07-software-architecture.md -o docs/diagrams/07-architecture.png 259 ``` 260 261 Output: one PNG per fenced ```mermaid block, named `07-architecture-{1..4}.png`. 262

"onWrite trigger" .-> cf 51 52 phone -- "HTTPS" --> auth 53 phone -- "streams + writes" --> fs 54 phone -- "callable RPC" --> cf 55 fcm -- "push" --> phone 56 57 classDef dev fill:#e0e7ff,stroke:#4f46e5,color:#1e1b4b 58 classDef cloud fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#a16207,color:#3f2d00 59 classDef gh fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#0c2d6b 60 classDef end_ fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#047857,color:#04372a 61 class dev1,dev2,dev3,dev4,dev5 dev 62 class auth,fs,cf,fcm,sched cloud 63 class repo,actions gh 64 class phone end_ 65 ``` 66 67 --- 68 69 ## 2. Sequence ??? happy-path delivery 70 71 ```mermaid 72 sequenceDiagram 73 actor U as Mali (user) 74 participant A as Flutter app 75 participant F as Firestore 76 participant Fn as Cloud Functions 77 participant FCM as FCM 78 actor Ad as Naree (admin) 79 80 U->>A: open Request page, pick items, submit 81 A->>Fn: submitRequest({items, address}) 82 Fn->>F: validate stock, create requests/{id} status=pending 83 Fn-->>A: 200 {requestId} 84 A-->>U: show in Queue: "Pending" 85 86 Fn->>FCM: notify all admins 87 FCM-->>Ad: push "new request" 88 89 Ad->>A: open Request Manage 90 A->>F: stream request + user profile 91 A-->>Ad: render 92 93 Ad->>Fn: approveRequest({reqId}) 94 Fn->>F: tx: stock-- ?? status=approved ?? return eligible drones 95 Fn-->>A: eligible[] 96 A-->>Ad: render drone picker 97 98 Ad->>Fn: assignDrone({reqId, droneId}) 99 Fn->>F: tx: create flight enroute ?? drone.status=flying ?? request.status=in_flight 100 Fn-->>A: ok 101 Fn->>FCM: notify user "dispatched" 102 FCM-->>U: push 103 104 Note over Fn,F: Scheduler every 60s 105 loop tickFlights 106 Fn->>F: query active flights 107 Fn->>Fn: compute progress + battery + dice 108 alt no failure & progress reaches 1.0 (enroute) 109 Fn->>F: flight.status=delivering 110 Fn->>FCM: notify user "arriving" 111 else next tick after delivering 112 Fn->>F: flight.status=completed ?? request.status=delivered 113 Fn->>FCM: notify user "confirm please" 114 end 115 end 116 117 U->>A: Confirm 118 A->>Fn: confirmDelivery({reqId}) 119 Fn->>F: request.status=confirmed ?? flight.status=returning ?? drone.status=flying 120 Note over Fn,F: ticks bring drone home 121 Fn->>F: drone.status=idle 122 ``` 123 124 --- 125 126 ## 3. Sequence ??? storm aborts mid-flight, admin reassigns 127 128 ```mermaid 129 sequenceDiagram 130 actor U as Mali (user) 131 participant A as Flutter app 132 participant F as Firestore 133 participant Fn as Cloud Functions 134 participant FCM as FCM 135 actor Ad as Naree (admin) 136 137 Note over Ad,Fn: Weather already set to "storm" earlier 138 U->>Fn: submitRequest 139 Fn->>F: requests/{id} status=pending 140 Ad->>Fn: approveRequest 141 Ad->>Fn: assignDrone(DRN-005) 142 Fn->>F: flight enroute, drone DRN-005 flying 143 144 loop tickFlights every 60s 145 Fn->>F: read weather=storm + flight 146 Fn->>Fn: rand() < 0.20 ??? ABORT 147 Fn->>F: flight.failureType=weather, status=aborted ?? request.status=failed ?? drone.status=idle 148 Fn->>FCM: notify user + admins 149 FCM-->>U: "flight aborted" 150 FCM-->>Ad: "reassign needed" 151 end 152 153 Ad->>A: open Request Manage (re-entry) 154 Ad->>Fn: setWeather("wind") 155 Fn->>F: weather/current state=wind 156 Ad->>Fn: assignDrone(DRN-002) 157 Fn->>F: new flight enroute (2nd attempt) ?? request.status=in_flight 158 159 loop tickFlights 160 Fn->>F: progress=1.0 ??? delivering ??? completed ?? request.status=delivered 161 Fn->>FCM: notify user 162 end 163 164 U->>Fn: confirmDelivery 165 Fn->>F: request.status=confirmed ?? flight returning 166 ``` 167 168 --- 169 170 ## 4. Request status state machine 171 172 ```mermaid 173 stateDiagram-v2 174 [*] --> pending : user submits 175 pending --> approved : admin approves 176 pending --> rejected : admin rejects 177 pending --> cancelled : user cancels 178 approved --> in_flight : admin assigns drone 179 in_flight --> delivered : tickFlights detects arrival 180 in_flight --> failed : sim rolls fail / battery / weather 181 failed --> in_flight : admin reassigns (new flight) 182 failed --> [*] : admin abandons (no reassign) 183 delivered --> confirmed : user taps Confirm 184 rejected --> [*] 185 cancelled --> [*] 186 confirmed --> [*] 187 ``` 188 189 --- 190 191 ## 5. Flight status state machine 192 193 ```mermaid 194 stateDiagram-v2 195 [*] --> enroute : assignDrone 196 enroute --> delivering : progress >= 1.0 197 enroute --> aborted : weather/battery/mech dice 198 delivering --> completed : next tick (60s hold) 199 completed --> returning : confirmDelivery fires 200 returning --> [*] : reaches base ??? drone idle 201 aborted --> [*] : drone returns or maintenance 202 ``` 203 204 --- 205 206 ## 6. Drone status state machine 207 208 ```mermaid 209 stateDiagram-v2 210 [*] --> idle : seed 211 idle --> flying : assignDrone 212 flying --> idle : flight returning completes OR aborted no damage 213 flying --> maintenance : aborted mechanical OR battery exhausted 214 idle --> maintenance : admin toggles 215 maintenance --> idle : admin toggles 216 idle --> offline : admin takes offline 217 offline --> idle : admin brings online 218 ``` 219 220 --- 221 222 ## 7. Entity-relationship (Firestore collections) 223 224 ```mermaid 225 erDiagram 226 USER ||--o{ REQUEST : "submits" 227 USER ||--o{ NOTIFICATION : "receives" 228 USER ||--o| ADDRESS : "has pin" 229 230 REQUEST }o--|| CATALOG_ITEM : "contains many" 231 REQUEST ||--o{ FLIGHT : "spawns attempts" 232 REQUEST }o--|| USER : "owned by" 233 234 DRONE ||--o{ FLIGHT : "assigned to" 235 236 FLIGHT }o--|| WEATHER : "snapshot at takeoff" 237 238 CATALOG_ITEM { 239 string itemId PK 240 string name 241 float weightKg 242 int stock 243 bool active 244 } 245 USER { 246 string uid PK 247 string nationalId UK 248 string name 249 string phone 250 string role 251 Address deliveryAddress 252 bool locked 253 } 254 REQUEST { 255 string reqId PK 256 string userId FK 257 array items 258 float totalWeightKg 259 string status 260 string priority 261 string currentFlightId FK 262 ts createdAt 263 } 264 DRONE { 265 string droneId PK 266 string status 267 int batteryPct 268 Coord baseLocation 269 float maxPayloadKg 270 string currentFlightId FK 271 } 272 FLIGHT { 273 string flightId PK 274 string droneId FK 275 string requestId FK 276 string status 277 Coord origin 278 Coord destination 279 ts takeoffAt 280 ts etaAt 281 float speedKmh 282 float weatherModifierAtTakeoff 283 int batteryAtTakeoff 284 string failureType 285 } 286 NOTIFICATION { 287 string nid PK 288 string type 289 string title 290 string body 291 string requestId FK 292 string flightId FK 293 ts readAt 294 } 295 WEATHER { 296 string state 297 ts updatedAt 298 string updatedBy FK 299 } 300 ``` 301 302 --- 303 304 ## 8. Render the PNGs 305 306 ```bash 307 npx --yes @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli -i docs/08-implementation-diagram.md -o docs/diagrams/08-implementation.png 308 ``` 309 310 Mermaid CLI emits one PNG per fenced block: `08-implementation-1.png` ??? `08-implementation-7.png`. 311

Linux

droneaid-csc291"] auth["Firebase Auth"] fs["Firestore"] cf["Cloud Functions

ABORT Fn->>F: flight.failureType=weather, status=aborted ?? request.status=failed ?? drone.status=idle Fn->>FCM: notify user + admins FCM-->>U: "flight aborted" FCM-->>Ad: "reassign needed" end Ad->>A: open Request Manage (re-entry) Ad->>Fn: setWeather("wind") Fn->>F: weather/current state=wind Ad->>Fn: assignDrone(DRN-002) Fn->>F: new flight enroute (2nd attempt) ?? request.status=in_flight loop tickFlights Fn->>F: progress=1.0 ??? delivering ??? completed ?? request.status=delivered Fn->>FCM: notify user end U->>Fn: confirmDelivery Fn->>F: request.status=confirmed ?? flight returning ``` --- ## 4. Request status state machine ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> pending : user submits pending --> approved : admin approves pending --> rejected : admin rejects pending --> cancelled : user cancels approved --> in_flight : admin assigns drone in_flight --> delivered : tickFlights detects arrival in_flight --> failed : sim rolls fail / battery / weather failed --> in_flight : admin reassigns (new flight) failed --> [*] : admin abandons (no reassign) delivered --> confirmed : user taps Confirm rejected --> [*] cancelled --> [*] confirmed --> [*] ``` --- ## 5. content.content:
1	# 10 ??? Prototype design notes
2	
3	Screenshots captured from the interactive prototype (`DroneAid Prototype.html`) at viewport **390 ?? 844** (iPhone-class portrait). Each section pairs a screenshot with the contract a developer needs to implement the page in Flutter against the existing `firestore.rules` + Cloud Functions.
4	
5	The page IDs (`P-U-XX` / `P-A-XX`) match `docs/09-page-flow-design.md`. Asset filenames are kept stable so a teammate can grep `P-U-04_queue.png` and find both the image and these notes.
6	
7	Shared chrome (every page):
8	- **App bar:** brand mark + "DroneAid" + role badge ("?? Admin" for staff).
9	- **Right-side icons:** light/dark toggle, notifications bell, profile avatar. The admin bar swaps the bell for a weather chip (current state) and a gear that deep-links to `P-A-06`.
10	- **Bottom nav (user):** Request ?? Queue ?? Tracking ?? History ?? Profile.
11	- **Top tabs (admin):** Requests ?? Drones ?? Control ?? More (More ??? Weather; Inventory reached programmatically via `actions.go("admin.inventory")`, so a real implementation needs an explicit link from the More tab ??? see P-A-07 notes).
12	- **Type / color:** Material-3-ish; primary teal `#1f6f6c`-ish, accent for "Urgent", warm yellow for "Pending", red for "Failed/Cancelled", grey-green for "Confirmed".
13	
14	> Implementation defaults referenced below: Riverpod for state, `go_router` routes per `09-page-flow-design.md`, `cloud_firestore` for reads, `cloud_functions` for writes (rules deny direct client writes on most collections).
15	
16	---
17	
18	## P-U-01 ??? Log in
19	
20	![Login](prototype-screens/user/P-U-01_login.png)
21	
22	**Purpose.** Auth gate. Sole entry point for unauthenticated users.
23	
24	**Layout.**
25	- Centered brand mark + wordmark + tagline "Relief delivery, on demand."
26	- Two text fields:
27	  - `13-digit national ID` (numeric keyboard, 13/13 counter on right).
28	  - `Password` with show/hide eye toggle.
29	- Primary CTA: `Log in` (filled, full width).
30	- Secondary link row: "New here?  Create an account ???" ??? `P-U-02`.
31	- Demo accounts footer card (prototype only ??? strip in prod builds).
32	
33	**Validation.**
34	- National ID exactly 13 digits; client-side check via existing `app/lib/utils/thai_id_validator.dart`.
35	- Password ??? 8 chars (Firebase Auth default).
36	
37	**Behaviour.** Sign in via Firebase Auth using the synthetic-email pattern `<nationalId>@drone-aid.local` (per spec ??5). On success, router redirects based on the `role` field of `users/{uid}`:
38	- `role: "user"` ??? `/user/home` (which renders P-U-03 in this prototype).
39	- `role: "admin"` ??? `/admin/requests` (P-A-01).
40	
41	**Errors.** Inline below password: "Wrong national ID or password." (never tell which one is wrong). Lock after 5 attempts for 60 s (matches spec ??5).
42	
43	---
44	
45	## P-U-02 ??? Create an account
46	
47	![Register](prototype-screens/user/P-U-02_register.png)
48	
49	**Purpose.** Self-serve user registration. Admins are never created from the app.
50	
51	**Layout.**
52	- Back arrow + "Create an account" header.
53	- Stacked fields: `Full name`, `13-digit national ID` (with counter), `Phone (+66???)`, `Password`, `Confirm password`.
54	- "I agree to the program terms." checkbox.
55	- Primary CTA `Create account` ??? **disabled until** all required fields are valid AND the checkbox is ticked.
56	
57	**Validation.** Same ID + phone regex (`^\+?\d{10,15}$`) used by the `updateProfile` callable in `functions/src/callable/updateProfile.ts`. Password and confirm must match.
58	
59	**Behaviour.** Calls `createUserWithEmailAndPassword(<nationalId>@drone-aid.local, password)`. The `onUserCreated` Auth trigger (`functions/src/triggers/onUserCreated.ts`) provisions the `users/{uid}` doc with `role: "user"` automatically. After provisioning resolves, router pushes to `/user/home`.
60	
61	---
62	
63	## P-U-03 ??? Request supplies (user home)
64	
65	![Request](prototype-screens/user/P-U-03_request.png)
66	
67	**Purpose.** Default landing tab for end users; build a cart and submit a relief request.
68	
69	**Sections.**
70	1. **Header.** Eyebrow `P-U-03 ?? REQUEST`, h1 "Request supplies", subtitle "Pick what you need, drop a pin, send a drone."
71	2. **Catalog list.** One row per `catalog/{itemId}` doc where `active == true`:
72	   - Coloured square icon + item name.
73	   - "`{weightKg} kg ?? {stock} in stock`" mono detail line.
74	   - Trailing `+` button ??? adds 1 to local cart (max 10 per item per `submitRequest` schema).
75	3. **Delivery pin card.** Pin icon + place label + "lat, lng" + `Edit` button (opens a map picker ??? wire to `flutter_map`, `latlong2`).
76	4. **Priority toggle.** Pill group `Normal` / `Urgent` (with warning icon). Default `Normal`.
77	5. **Submit CTA.** "Add items to submit" ??? copy switches to "Submit request" once cart is non-empty; disabled when no items selected, when total weight > 6.0 kg, or when any selected item just went out of stock.
78	
79	**Behaviour.** On submit, call `submitRequest` callable. On success, snackbar "Request sent ??? DroneAid is reviewing." and pop to P-U-04 (Queue) with the new card visible.
80	
81	**Empty / error states.**
82	- Catalog empty (`active` list returns nothing): show a soft empty state "No items available right now."
83	- Network error: snackbar + retry on the catalog query.
84	
85	---
86	
87	## P-U-04 ??? Your queue (live updates)
88	
89	![Queue](prototype-screens/user/P-U-04_queue.png)
90	
91	**Purpose.** Live status of the user's own active requests; one tap from any of them into Tracking.
92	
93	**Layout.**
94	- Header "Your queue", subtitle "Submitted requests update in real time as admins assign and drones fly."
95	- **ACTIVE** section: one card per request whose status is in `{pending, approved, in_flight, delivered}`. Each card shows ID, status pill, items summary, weight, delivery label, and ??? if `currentFlightId` is set ??? a metrics row (ETA ?? Battery ?? Drone) + `Track` button ??? `/user/tracking/{flightId}`.
96	- **PENDING** section (compact): pending requests with an `Urgent` chip if relevant; trailing `Cancel` text button (only while status is `pending`).
97	- Footer link "See past deliveries ???" jumps to P-U-07.
98	
99	**Data.** Firestore listener on `requests` where `userId == uid` and `status in [pending, approved, in_flight, delivered]`, ordered by `createdAt desc`.
100	
101	**Actions.**
102	- `Cancel` ??? `cancelRequest` callable (admin allowed any time before in_flight; user allowed only while `pending`).
103	- `Track` ??? push tracking route (renders P-U-05).
104	
105	---
106	
107	## P-U-05 ??? Tracking flight
108	
109	![Tracking](prototype-screens/user/P-U-05_tracking.png)
110	
111	**Purpose.** Live in-flight view for a single flight.
112	
113	**Layout (fullscreen, no bottom nav).**
114	- Back arrow + "Tracking #{reqId}".
115	- **Map.** `flutter_map` w/ OSM tiles; markers for origin (BASE), destination pin, drone marker animated along the path. Path: dotted polyline from base ??? destination, solid behind the drone marker as it advances.
116	- **Status banner.** "??? In flight" (or "Delivering", "Returning", "Aborted") + drone ID on the right.
117	- **Metrics grid (2 cols ?? 3 rows):** ETA, Arrival (clock time), Battery, Speed, Distance left.
118	- **Cargo card.** Each item with icon, name, ??qty.
119	
120	**Data.** Snapshot listener on `flights/{flightId}`; the live metrics derive from `snapshot()` in `functions/src/lib/sim.ts` (server) ??? the client computes the visible position from `takeoffAt`, `speedKmh`, `weatherModifierAtTakeoff`, and now() so the UI doesn't have to wait for the per-minute `tickFlights` write.
121	
122	**Edge cases.**
123	- Flight aborted: status pill goes red, banner copy "Flight aborted ??? reason"; map drone marker fades; bottom shows "Awaiting reassignment" hint and back-button takes the user to P-U-04.
124	
125	---
126	
127	## P-U-06 ??? Confirm receipt (Finalize)
128	
129	![Confirm](prototype-screens/user/P-U-06_confirm.png)
130	
131	**Purpose.** Close the loop after a delivery; required before the drone goes "returning" and the request marks `confirmed`.
132	
133	**Layout (fullscreen modal-feel).**
134	- Back arrow + "Confirm receipt".
135	- Hero: rounded square package icon with a green check badge.
136	- Eyebrow `P-U-06 ?? FINALIZE` + h1 "Did you receive your supplies?".
137	- **Delivery summary card.** Item rows with ??qty. Footer line "Delivered at {time} by DRN-{id}".
138	- Primary CTA `Yes, confirm` ??? `confirmDelivery` callable.
139	- Secondary `Something's wrong ??? report` ??? opens a small dialog with a textarea + submit (this is a placeholder for the dispute flow ??? out of scope for v1, but the button must exist per the prototype).
140	
141	**Entry points.** Push notification deep-link `/user/confirm/{reqId}`, or tap on the matching card in P-U-04 once status is `delivered`.
142	
143	---
144	
145	## P-U-07 ??? History (past deliveries)
146	
147	![History](prototype-screens/user/P-U-07_history.png)
148	
149	**Purpose.** Read-only audit of every request the user has made.
150	
151	**Layout.**
152	- Day-grouped list ("Yesterday", "May 20", ???) of compact cards: request ID, status pill (`Confirmed` / `Cancelled` / `Failed`), items summary, weight, delivery label, and ??? when applicable ??? a small footnote ("2 flights (1 aborted)") so the user can see retried deliveries.
153	
154	**Data.** Same `requests` listener as P-U-04 but filtered to `status in [confirmed, cancelled, failed]`. Group by `createdAt` calendar day in the device's local time.
155	
156	**Detail view.** Tapping a card pushes a read-only request detail (re-use the layout of P-U-04's active card but with all metrics frozen at completion time). Not separately documented ??? uses the same widget tree.
157	
158	---
159	
160	## P-U-08 ??? Notifications
161	
162	![Notifications](prototype-screens/user/P-U-08_notifications.png)
163	
164	**Purpose.** All FCM notifications surfaced in-app so the user can re-read them.
165	
166	**Layout.**
167	- Back arrow + "Notifications".
168	- Sectioned list: `Earlier` (24h+), `Today` (within last 24h). Each row: title (bold), body, timestamp ("30 min ago", "1 d ago"). Rows with a deep-link have a chevron on the right.
169	
170	**Data.** Local cache of received FCM messages plus a server-side mirror at `users/{uid}/notifications/{id}`. Marking-as-read = update `readAt` via a lightweight callable (`markNotifRead` in the prototype) ??? not yet implemented server-side.
171	
172	---
173	
174	## P-U-09 ??? Profile + settings
175	
176	![Profile](prototype-screens/user/P-U-09_profile.png)
177	
178	**Purpose.** Self-service edits + theme/notification toggles + logout.
179	
180	**Layout.**
181	- Header card: avatar circle ("MS" initials), name, USER badge, national ID, phone.
182	- **PROFILE** card: `Full name`, `Phone`, `Delivery pin` ??? each with an inline `Edit` action.
183	- **SETTINGS** card:
184	  - `Theme` row with `Light` / `Dark` pill (binds to `prefs.theme`).
185	  - `Language` row (read-only "English (only v1)").
186	  - `Notifications` row with a `Manage` action (opens OS-level settings via `permission_handler` in a real build).
187	- **ACCOUNT** card: `Account ID` (read-only), bottom-aligned destructive `Log out` button.
188	
189	### P-U-09 a ??? Log-out confirmation dialog
190	
191	![Logout dialog](prototype-screens/user/P-U-09a_logout_dialog.png)
192	
193	Modal: "Log out?" + body "You'll need to sign in again to submit or track requests." + actions `Cancel` (text) and `Log out` (destructive). Show as a Material `AlertDialog` (not a bottom sheet) to match the prototype.
194	
195	**Data.** All field edits go through `updateProfile` callable (already implemented). The callable accepts a sparse patch, so the page only sends fields the user actually changed.
196	
197	---
198	
199	## P-A-01 ??? Triage (admin requests)
200	
201	![Admin Requests](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-01_admin_requests.png)
202	
203	**Purpose.** Admin's default landing tab ??? live triage feed.
204	
205	**Layout.**
206	- Header `P-A-01 ?? TRIAGE`, h1 "Requests", subtitle "{n} pending ?? live feed from users."
207	- **Filter row:** `All` (default, primary chip) / `Pending` / `In flight` / `Delivered` / `Failed`.
208	- **Sort:** trailing pill ??? `Newest` (default).
209	- **List.** One card per request: small ID, status pill (+ `URGENT` chip if `priority === "urgent"`), timestamp, requester avatar/name, items summary, weight, delivery label. The Requests badge in the top tab shows the count of currently `pending` requests.
210	
211	**Data.** Firestore listener on `requests` ordered by `createdAt desc`. The "Pending" count drives the badge.
212	
213	**Actions.** Tap a card ??? P-A-02.
214	
215	---
216	
217	## P-A-02 ??? Request manage
218	
219	![Manage Request](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-02_request_manage.png)
220	
221	**Purpose.** Approve, reject, or reassign a single request; trigger drone dispatch.
222	
223	**Layout.**
224	- Breadcrumb back ("??? Requests") + eyebrow `P-A-02 ?? MANAGE` + h1 "Request #{reqId}".
225	- Status pill + "?? Submitted {date}, {time}".
226	- **REQUESTER** card: avatar, name, national ID, phone, on the right "{n} prior ??? {x} ok ?? {y} failed" (denormalized counters).
227	- **ITEMS** card: one row per item (icon + name + "{kg} kg ??{qty}"); footer "Total weight  {kg} kg".
228	- **DELIVERY** card: pin icon + place label + lat/lng on the left, "FROM BASE ??? {km} km" on the right; below it a static mini-map. Distance comes from `haversineKm` in `functions/src/lib/geo.ts` (or recomputed client-side for the map).
229	- **ACTION** card (below the fold): primary `Approve` (filled, opens drone-selection sheet), secondary `Reject` (outlined, opens reason input). For an `approved` or `failed` request, the primary becomes `Assign drone` and opens the eligible-drone list from `approveRequest`'s response.
230	
231	**Data + writes.** Reads `requests/{reqId}` + the user doc + each `catalog/{id}`. Writes via callables: `approveRequest`, `rejectRequest`, `assignDrone`.
232	
233	**Hidden states.**
234	- Out-of-stock at approve time ??? callable throws `failed-precondition`; show a snackbar and refresh the items section.
235	- No eligible drones ??? empty state in the assign sheet with a hint "Bring a drone idle, then retry."
236	
237	---
238	
239	## P-A-03 ??? Fleet (drones)
240	
241	![Drones](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-03_drones.png)
242	
243	**Purpose.** Inventory of every drone with battery + status at a glance.
244	
245	**Layout.**
246	- Header `P-A-03 ?? FLEET`, h1 "Drones", subtitle "{idle} idle ?? {flying} flying ?? {maint.} maint. ?? {offline} offline".
247	- **Filter row:** `All` / `Idle` / `Flying` / `Maintenance` / `Offline`.
248	- **Drone cards** (tappable to P-A-04):
249	  - Drone icon + ID + "max {kg} kg".
250	  - Status pill (right): `Idle` (green), `Flying` (teal), `Maint.` (yellow), `Offline` (grey).
251	  - Battery row: thin bar + percent number on the right.
252	  - Flying cards add a footer: "{reqId} ?? ETA in {min}".
253	
254	**Data.** Firestore listener on `drones` (small collection, no pagination needed).
255	
256	---
257	
258	## P-A-04 ??? Drone detail
259	
260	![Drone detail](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-04_drone_detail.png)
261	
262	**Purpose.** Per-drone telemetry + manual ops controls.
263	
264	**Layout.**
265	- Back chip "??? Drones" + drone icon + bold ID + status pill.
266	- **BATTERY** row: full-width bar + percent.
267	- 2??2 metric grid: `PAYLOAD` (max kg), `BASE` (lat, lng), `LAST SEEN` (relative), `DRONE ID` (raw doc ID, mono).
268	- **RECENT FLIGHTS** card: empty state when none ("No flights yet."), otherwise the last 5 flights with status + time.
269	- **ACTIONS** card: two outlined buttons ??? `Take offline` (toggles `status: offline`), `Maintenance` (toggles `status: maintenance`). Both call `toggleDroneMaintenance` / a sibling `toggleDroneOffline` callable.
270	
271	**Rules.** Direct writes to `drones` are denied; all transitions go through callables, which themselves enforce that an `in_flight`-bound drone can't be flipped offline without first aborting the flight.
272	
273	---
274	
275	## P-A-05 ??? Control (live map)
276	
277	![Control map](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-05_control_map.png)
278	
279	**Purpose.** One unified map of every active flight and base.
280	
281	**Layout.**
282	- Top strip on the right: `{n} active` pill + current weather chip (`Clear` / `Wind` / `Storm`).
283	- **Map** fills the rest of the screen. Markers: each idle drone at its base (small grey icon), each active flight (teal animated marker on its polyline), one `BASE` label. Polylines: dotted ahead, solid behind the drone marker.
284	- Footer hint pill "Tap a drone to open details" ??? tapping a drone marker pushes P-A-04.
285	
286	**Data.** Combine the `flights` listener (active statuses only) with the `drones` listener. Pre-compute marker positions client-side using the same `snapshot()` math as P-U-05 so the marker animates between server ticks.
287	
288	---
289	
290	## P-A-06 ??? Operational weather
291	
292	![Weather](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-06_weather.png)
293	
294	**Purpose.** One global weather state that adjusts speed, battery drain and abort risk for every flight.
295	
296	**Layout.**
297	- Header `P-A-06 ?? OPERATIONAL WEATHER`, h1 "Weather", subtitle "Affects speed, battery drain, and abort risk for all in-flight drones."
298	- **Current state card:** large pill on the left ("??? Clear") + "Current: clear" + "Updated {time} by {admin}" on the right.
299	- **SET STATE** radio group:
300	  - `Clear` ??? `Mod ??1.0 ?? drain 80%/hr`
301	  - `Wind` ??? `Mod ??0.7 ?? drain 100%/hr ?? 3% abort risk`
302	  - `Storm` ??? `Mod ??0.0 ?? drain 120%/hr ?? 20% abort risk`
303	- Filled `Save` button at the bottom; **disabled while the draft equals the current state** (so it can't be no-op-submitted).
304	
305	**Data + writes.** Reads `weather/current`. Writes via `setWeather` callable ??? admin-only by rules. The next `tickFlights` run applies the new modifier to every active flight in its math.
306	
307	---
308	
309	## P-A-07 ??? Stock control (inventory)
310	
311	![Inventory](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-07_inventory.png)
312	
313	**Purpose.** Restock the catalog and toggle items active/inactive.
314	
315	**Layout.**
316	- Header `P-A-07 ?? STOCK CONTROL`, h1 "Inventory", top-right outlined `+ Add item` button.
317	- **Item rows** (one per `catalog/{id}`): coloured square icon, name + "{kg} kg ?? Active/Inactive" caption, large in-stock number, `+ Restock` button (opens a small numeric dialog), `Deactivate` link button.
318	
319	**Data + writes.**
320	- `restockItem` callable adds an integer to `stock` (server-side `FieldValue.increment`).
321	- `toggleCatalogActive` flips `active`. Deactivated items are still shown here but vanish from P-U-03.
322	- `createCatalogItem` for new items.
323	
324	**Open issue.** In the prototype, the "More" tab opens Weather, and Inventory is only reachable by calling `actions.go("admin.inventory")`. The real implementation needs an explicit Inventory link ??? easiest is to make the "More" tab open a small sheet/menu with `Weather`, `Inventory`, `Profile`, instead of routing straight to Weather.
325	
326	---

# 10 ??? Prototype design notes

Screenshots captured from the interactive prototype (`DroneAid Prototype.html`) at viewport **390 ?? 844** (iPhone-class portrait). Each section pairs a screenshot with the contract a developer needs to implement the page in Flutter against the existing `firestore.rules` + Cloud Functions.

The page IDs (`P-U-XX` / `P-A-XX`) match `docs/09-page-flow-design.md`. Asset filenames are kept stable so a teammate can grep `P-U-04_queue.png` and find both the image and these notes.

Shared chrome (every page):
- **App bar:** brand mark + "DroneAid" + role badge ("?? Admin" for staff).
- **Right-side icons:** light/dark toggle, notifications bell, profile avatar. The admin bar swaps the bell for a weather chip (current state) and a gear that deep-links to `P-A-06`.
- **Bottom nav (user):** Request ?? Queue ?? Tracking ?? History ?? Profile.
- **Top tabs (admin):** Requests ?? Drones ?? Control ?? More (More ??? Weather; Inventory reached programmatically via `actions.go("admin.inventory")`, so a real implementation needs an explicit link from the More tab ??? see P-A-07 notes).
- **Type / color:** Material-3-ish; primary teal `#1f6f6c`-ish, accent for "Urgent", warm yellow for "Pending", red for "Failed/Cancelled", grey-green for "Confirmed".

> Implementation defaults referenced below: Riverpod for state, `go_router` routes per `09-page-flow-design.md`, `cloud_firestore` for reads, `cloud_functions` for writes (rules deny direct client writes on most collections).

---

## P-U-01 ??? Log in

![Login](prototype-screens/user/P-U-01_login.png)

**Purpose.** Auth gate. Sole entry point for unauthenticated users.

**Layout.**
- Centered brand mark + wordmark + tagline "Relief delivery, on demand."
- Two text fields:
  - `13-digit national ID` (numeric keyboard, 13/13 counter on right).
  - `Password` with show/hide eye toggle.
- Primary CTA: `Log in` (filled, full width).
- Secondary link row: "New here?  Create an account ???" ??? `P-U-02`.
- Demo accounts footer card (prototype only ??? strip in prod builds).

**Validation.**
- National ID exactly 13 digits; client-side check via existing `app/lib/utils/thai_id_validator.dart`.
- Password ??? 8 chars (Firebase Auth default).

**Behaviour.** Sign in via Firebase Auth using the synthetic-email pattern `<nationalId>@drone-aid.local` (per spec ??5). On success, router redirects based on the `role` field of `users/{uid}`:
- `role: "user"` ??? `/user/home` (which renders P-U-03 in this prototype).
- `role: "admin"` ??? `/admin/requests` (P-A-01).

**Errors.** Inline below password: "Wrong national ID or password." (never tell which one is wrong). Lock after 5 attempts for 60 s (matches spec ??5).

---

## P-U-02 ??? Create an account

![Register](prototype-screens/user/P-U-02_register.png)

**Purpose.** Self-serve user registration. Admins are never created from the app.

**Layout.**
- Back arrow + "Create an account" header.
- Stacked fields: `Full name`, `13-digit national ID` (with counter), `Phone (+66???)`, `Password`, `Confirm password`.
- "I agree to the program terms." checkbox.
- Primary CTA `Create account` ??? **disabled until** all required fields are valid AND the checkbox is ticked.

**Validation.** Same ID + phone regex (`^\+?\d{10,15}$`) used by the `updateProfile` callable in `functions/src/callable/updateProfile.ts`. Password and confirm must match.

**Behaviour.** Calls `createUserWithEmailAndPassword(<nationalId>@drone-aid.local, password)`. The `onUserCreated` Auth trigger (`functions/src/triggers/onUserCreated.ts`) provisions the `users/{uid}` doc with `role: "user"` automatically. After provisioning resolves, router pushes to `/user/home`.

---

## P-U-03 ??? Request supplies (user home)

![Request](prototype-screens/user/P-U-03_request.png)

**Purpose.** Default landing tab for end users; build a cart and submit a relief request.

**Sections.**
1. **Header.** Eyebrow `P-U-03 ?? REQUEST`, h1 "Request supplies", subtitle "Pick what you need, drop a pin, send a drone."
2. **Catalog list.** One row per `catalog/{itemId}` doc where `active == true`:
   - Coloured square icon + item name.
   - "`{weightKg} kg ?? {stock} in stock`" mono detail line.
   - Trailing `+` button ??? adds 1 to local cart (max 10 per item per `submitRequest` schema).
3. **Delivery pin card.** Pin icon + place label + "lat, lng" + `Edit` button (opens a map picker ??? wire to `flutter_map`, `latlong2`).
4. **Priority toggle.** Pill group `Normal` / `Urgent` (with warning icon). Default `Normal`.
5. **Submit CTA.** "Add items to submit" ??? copy switches to "Submit request" once cart is non-empty; disabled when no items selected, when total weight > 6.0 kg, or when any selected item just went out of stock.

**Behaviour.** On submit, call `submitRequest` callable. On success, snackbar "Request sent ??? DroneAid is reviewing." and pop to P-U-04 (Queue) with the new card visible.

**Empty / error states.**
- Catalog empty (`active` list returns nothing): show a soft empty state "No items available right now."
- Network error: snackbar + retry on the catalog query.

---

## P-U-04 ??? Your queue (live updates)

![Queue](prototype-screens/user/P-U-04_queue.png)

**Purpose.** Live status of the user's own active requests; one tap from any of them into Tracking.

**Layout.**
- Header "Your queue", subtitle "Submitted requests update in real time as admins assign and drones fly."
- **ACTIVE** section: one card per request whose status is in `{pending, approved, in_flight, delivered}`. Each card shows ID, status pill, items summary, weight, delivery label, and ??? if `currentFlightId` is set ??? a metrics row (ETA ?? Battery ?? Drone) + `Track` button ??? `/user/tracking/{flightId}`.
- **PENDING** section (compact): pending requests with an `Urgent` chip if relevant; trailing `Cancel` text button (only while status is `pending`).
- Footer link "See past deliveries ???" jumps to P-U-07.

**Data.** Firestore listener on `requests` where `userId == uid` and `status in [pending, approved, in_flight, delivered]`, ordered by `createdAt desc`.

**Actions.**
- `Cancel` ??? `cancelRequest` callable (admin allowed any time before in_flight; user allowed only while `pending`).
- `Track` ??? push tracking route (renders P-U-05).

---

## P-U-05 ??? Tracking flight

![Tracking](prototype-screens/user/P-U-05_tracking.png)

**Purpose.** Live in-flight view for a single flight.

**Layout (fullscreen, no bottom nav).**
- Back arrow + "Tracking #{reqId}".
- **Map.** `flutter_map` w/ OSM tiles; markers for origin (BASE), destination pin, drone marker animated along the path. Path: dotted polyline from base ??? destination, solid behind the drone marker as it advances.
- **Status banner.** "??? In flight" (or "Delivering", "Returning", "Aborted") + drone ID on the right.
- **Metrics grid (2 cols ?? 3 rows):** ETA, Arrival (clock time), Battery, Speed, Distance left.
- **Cargo card.** Each item with icon, name, ??qty.

**Data.** Snapshot listener on `flights/{flightId}`; the live metrics derive from `snapshot()` in `functions/src/lib/sim.ts` (server) ??? the client computes the visible position from `takeoffAt`, `speedKmh`, `weatherModifierAtTakeoff`, and now() so the UI doesn't have to wait for the per-minute `tickFlights` write.

**Edge cases.**
- Flight aborted: status pill goes red, banner copy "Flight aborted ??? reason"; map drone marker fades; bottom shows "Awaiting reassignment" hint and back-button takes the user to P-U-04.

---

## P-U-06 ??? Confirm receipt (Finalize)

![Confirm](prototype-screens/user/P-U-06_confirm.png)

**Purpose.** Close the loop after a delivery; required before the drone goes "returning" and the request marks `confirmed`.

**Layout (fullscreen modal-feel).**
- Back arrow + "Confirm receipt".
- Hero: rounded square package icon with a green check badge.
- Eyebrow `P-U-06 ?? FINALIZE` + h1 "Did you receive your supplies?".
- **Delivery summary card.** Item rows with ??qty. Footer line "Delivered at {time} by DRN-{id}".
- Primary CTA `Yes, confirm` ??? `confirmDelivery` callable.
- Secondary `Something's wrong ??? report` ??? opens a small dialog with a textarea + submit (this is a placeholder for the dispute flow ??? out of scope for v1, but the button must exist per the prototype).

**Entry points.** Push notification deep-link `/user/confirm/{reqId}`, or tap on the matching card in P-U-04 once status is `delivered`.

---

## P-U-07 ??? History (past deliveries)

![History](prototype-screens/user/P-U-07_history.png)

**Purpose.** Read-only audit of every request the user has made.

**Layout.**
- Day-grouped list ("Yesterday", "May 20", ???) of compact cards: request ID, status pill (`Confirmed` / `Cancelled` / `Failed`), items summary, weight, delivery label, and ??? when applicable ??? a small footnote ("2 flights (1 aborted)") so the user can see retried deliveries.

**Data.** Same `requests` listener as P-U-04 but filtered to `status in [confirmed, cancelled, failed]`. Group by `createdAt` calendar day in the device's local time.

**Detail view.** Tapping a card pushes a read-only request detail (re-use the layout of P-U-04's active card but with all metrics frozen at completion time). Not separately documented ??? uses the same widget tree.

---

## P-U-08 ??? Notifications

![Notifications](prototype-screens/user/P-U-08_notifications.png)

**Purpose.** All FCM notifications surfaced in-app so the user can re-read them.

**Layout.**
- Back arrow + "Notifications".
- Sectioned list: `Earlier` (24h+), `Today` (within last 24h). Each row: title (bold), body, timestamp ("30 min ago", "1 d ago"). Rows with a deep-link have a chevron on the right.

**Data.** Local cache of received FCM messages plus a server-side mirror at `users/{uid}/notifications/{id}`. Marking-as-read = update `readAt` via a lightweight callable (`markNotifRead` in the prototype) ??? not yet implemented server-side.

---

## P-U-09 ??? Profile + settings

![Profile](prototype-screens/user/P-U-09_profile.png)

**Purpose.** Self-service edits + theme/notification toggles + logout.

**Layout.**
- Header card: avatar circle ("MS" initials), name, USER badge, national ID, phone.
- **PROFILE** card: `Full name`, `Phone`, `Delivery pin` ??? each with an inline `Edit` action.
- **SETTINGS** card:
  - `Theme` row with `Light` / `Dark` pill (binds to `prefs.theme`).
  - `Language` row (read-only "English (only v1)").
  - `Notifications` row with a `Manage` action (opens OS-level settings via `permission_handler` in a real build).
- **ACCOUNT** card: `Account ID` (read-only), bottom-aligned destructive `Log out` button.

### P-U-09 a ??? Log-out confirmation dialog

![Logout dialog](prototype-screens/user/P-U-09a_logout_dialog.png)

Modal: "Log out?" + body "You'll need to sign in again to submit or track requests." + actions `Cancel` (text) and `Log out` (destructive). Show as a Material `AlertDialog` (not a bottom sheet) to match the prototype.

**Data.** All field edits go through `updateProfile` callable (already implemented). The callable accepts a sparse patch, so the page only sends fields the user actually changed.

---

## P-A-01 ??? Triage (admin requests)

![Admin Requests](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-01_admin_requests.png)

**Purpose.** Admin's default landing tab ??? live triage feed.

**Layout.**
- Header `P-A-01 ?? TRIAGE`, h1 "Requests", subtitle "{n} pending ?? live feed from users."
- **Filter row:** `All` (default, primary chip) / `Pending` / `In flight` / `Delivered` / `Failed`.
- **Sort:** trailing pill ??? `Newest` (default).
- **List.** One card per request: small ID, status pill (+ `URGENT` chip if `priority === "urgent"`), timestamp, requester avatar/name, items summary, weight, delivery label. The Requests badge in the top tab shows the count of currently `pending` requests.

**Data.** Firestore listener on `requests` ordered by `createdAt desc`. The "Pending" count drives the badge.

**Actions.** Tap a card ??? P-A-02.

---

## P-A-02 ??? Request manage

![Manage Request](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-02_request_manage.png)

**Purpose.** Approve, reject, or reassign a single request; trigger drone dispatch.

**Layout.**
- Breadcrumb back ("??? Requests") + eyebrow `P-A-02 ?? MANAGE` + h1 "Request #{reqId}".
- Status pill + "?? Submitted {date}, {time}".
- **REQUESTER** card: avatar, name, national ID, phone, on the right "{n} prior ??? {x} ok ?? {y} failed" (denormalized counters).
- **ITEMS** card: one row per item (icon + name + "{kg} kg ??{qty}"); footer "Total weight  {kg} kg".
- **DELIVERY** card: pin icon + place label + lat/lng on the left, "FROM BASE ??? {km} km" on the right; below it a static mini-map. Distance comes from `haversineKm` in `functions/src/lib/geo.ts` (or recomputed client-side for the map).
- **ACTION** card (below the fold): primary `Approve` (filled, opens drone-selection sheet), secondary `Reject` (outlined, opens reason input). For an `approved` or `failed` request, the primary becomes `Assign drone` and opens the eligible-drone list from `approveRequest`'s response.

**Data + writes.** Reads `requests/{reqId}` + the user doc + each `catalog/{id}`. Writes via callables: `approveRequest`, `rejectRequest`, `assignDrone`.

**Hidden states.**
- Out-of-stock at approve time ??? callable throws `failed-precondition`; show a snackbar and refresh the items section.
- No eligible drones ??? empty state in the assign sheet with a hint "Bring a drone idle, then retry."

---

## P-A-03 ??? Fleet (drones)

![Drones](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-03_drones.png)

**Purpose.** Inventory of every drone with battery + status at a glance.

**Layout.**
- Header `P-A-03 ?? FLEET`, h1 "Drones", subtitle "{idle} idle ?? {flying} flying ?? {maint.} maint. ?? {offline} offline".
- **Filter row:** `All` / `Idle` / `Flying` / `Maintenance` / `Offline`.
- **Drone cards** (tappable to P-A-04):
  - Drone icon + ID + "max {kg} kg".
  - Status pill (right): `Idle` (green), `Flying` (teal), `Maint.` (yellow), `Offline` (grey).
  - Battery row: thin bar + percent number on the right.
  - Flying cards add a footer: "{reqId} ?? ETA in {min}".

**Data.** Firestore listener on `drones` (small collection, no pagination needed).

---

## P-A-04 ??? Drone detail

![Drone detail](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-04_drone_detail.png)

**Purpose.** Per-drone telemetry + manual ops controls.

**Layout.**
- Back chip "??? Drones" + drone icon + bold ID + status pill.
- **BATTERY** row: full-width bar + percent.
- 2??2 metric grid: `PAYLOAD` (max kg), `BASE` (lat, lng), `LAST SEEN` (relative), `DRONE ID` (raw doc ID, mono).
- **RECENT FLIGHTS** card: empty state when none ("No flights yet."), otherwise the last 5 flights with status + time.
- **ACTIONS** card: two outlined buttons ??? `Take offline` (toggles `status: offline`), `Maintenance` (toggles `status: maintenance`). Both call `toggleDroneMaintenance` / a sibling `toggleDroneOffline` callable.

**Rules.** Direct writes to `drones` are denied; all transitions go through callables, which themselves enforce that an `in_flight`-bound drone can't be flipped offline without first aborting the flight.

---

## P-A-05 ??? Control (live map)

![Control map](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-05_control_map.png)

**Purpose.** One unified map of every active flight and base.

**Layout.**
- Top strip on the right: `{n} active` pill + current weather chip (`Clear` / `Wind` / `Storm`).
- **Map** fills the rest of the screen. Markers: each idle drone at its base (small grey icon), each active flight (teal animated marker on its polyline), one `BASE` label. Polylines: dotted ahead, solid behind the drone marker.
- Footer hint pill "Tap a drone to open details" ??? tapping a drone marker pushes P-A-04.

**Data.** Combine the `flights` listener (active statuses only) with the `drones` listener. Pre-compute marker positions client-side using the same `snapshot()` math as P-U-05 so the marker animates between server ticks.

---

## P-A-06 ??? Operational weather

![Weather](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-06_weather.png)

**Purpose.** One global weather state that adjusts speed, battery drain and abort risk for every flight.

**Layout.**
- Header `P-A-06 ?? OPERATIONAL WEATHER`, h1 "Weather", subtitle "Affects speed, battery drain, and abort risk for all in-flight drones."
- **Current state card:** large pill on the left ("??? Clear") + "Current: clear" + "Updated {time} by {admin}" on the right.
- **SET STATE** radio group:
  - `Clear` ??? `Mod ??1.0 ?? drain 80%/hr`
  - `Wind` ??? `Mod ??0.7 ?? drain 100%/hr ?? 3% abort risk`
  - `Storm` ??? `Mod ??0.0 ?? drain 120%/hr ?? 20% abort risk`
- Filled `Save` button at the bottom; **disabled while the draft equals the current state** (so it can't be no-op-submitted).

**Data + writes.** Reads `weather/current`. Writes via `setWeather` callable ??? admin-only by rules. The next `tickFlights` run applies the new modifier to every active flight in its math.

---

## P-A-07 ??? Stock control (inventory)

![Inventory](prototype-screens/admin/P-A-07_inventory.png)

**Purpose.** Restock the catalog and toggle items active/inactive.

**Layout.**
- Header `P-A-07 ?? STOCK CONTROL`, h1 "Inventory", top-right outlined `+ Add item` button.
- **Item rows** (one per `catalog/{id}`): coloured square icon, name + "{kg} kg ?? Active/Inactive" caption, large in-stock number, `+ Restock` button (opens a small numeric dialog), `Deactivate` link button.

**Data + writes.**
- `restockItem` callable adds an integer to `stock` (server-side `FieldValue.increment`).
- `toggleCatalogActive` flips `active`. Deactivated items are still shown here but vanish from P-U-03.
- `createCatalogItem` for new items.

**Open issue.** In the prototype, the "More" tab opens Weather, and Inventory is only reachable by calling `actions.go("admin.inventory")`. The real implementation needs an explicit Inventory link ??? easiest is to make the "More" tab open a small sheet/menu with `Weather`, `Inventory`, `Profile`, instead of routing straight to Weather.

---

1	# Native Windows is the standard dev environment for non-lead devs
2	
3	4 of 5 team members run Windows; only the lead (Aok) runs Linux. To avoid debugging "works on my machine" issues during a 12-day class project, we standardised the 4 Windows devs on **native Windows + Android Studio + Flutter SDK + Firebase CLI Windows binary**, rather than WSL2 or a hybrid setup. WSL2 was rejected because Android emulator GPU passthrough is unreliable and most devs would end up running AVD on the Windows side anyway, producing a hybrid mess; native Windows trades a duplicated PowerShell-equivalent setup section (`docs/setup-windows.md`) for one consistent path the whole team can follow.
4	
5	## Consequences
6	
7	- README setup section must be maintained in two flavours (Linux/macOS and Windows).
8	- `DRONE_AID_HANDLE` env var documented for both `~/.bashrc` (Linux) and Windows system env / PowerShell profile.
9	- Helper scripts in `scripts/` must stay portable Python; new bash-only scripts require a PowerShell equivalent.
10	- The `SessionEnd` Claude Code hook that copies session JSONL into `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` must work on Windows paths (`%USERPROFILE%\.claude`) as well as Linux (`~/.claude`).
11

file.content:
# Native Windows is the standard dev environment for non-lead devs

4 of 5 team members run Windows; only the lead (Aok) runs Linux. To avoid debugging "works on my machine" issues during a 12-day class project, we standardised the 4 Windows devs on **native Windows + Android Studio + Flutter SDK + Firebase CLI Windows binary**, rather than WSL2 or a hybrid setup. WSL2 was rejected because Android emulator GPU passthrough is unreliable and most devs would end up running AVD on the Windows side anyway, producing a hybrid mess; native Windows trades a duplicated PowerShell-equivalent setup section (`docs/setup-windows.md`) for one consistent path the whole team can follow.

## Consequences

- README setup section must be maintained in two flavours (Linux/macOS and Windows).
- `DRONE_AID_HANDLE` env var documented for both `~/.bashrc` (Linux) and Windows system env / PowerShell profile.
- Helper scripts in `scripts/` must stay portable Python; new bash-only scripts require a PowerShell equivalent.
- The `SessionEnd` Claude Code hook that copies session JSONL into `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` must work on Windows paths (`%USERPROFILE%\.claude`) as well as Linux (`~/.claude`).

1	# Project "done" = all promised features working on the emulator, FCM-push-to-device exempt
2	
3	The class submission deadline is 2026-06-05 (deliver to professor) with the demo window closing 2026-06-04 ??? a 14-day project window starting 2026-05-22. We committed to **full feature parity with the promised behaviour in `docs/09-page-flow-design.md` and `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-drone-relief-design.md`**, including the simulation polish that lower-scope options would have cut (failure dice, weather effects on flight, admin restock/maintenance flows). The one explicit carve-out is **FCM push notifications to a real device**: the Firebase emulator does not deliver push notifications, so server-side FCM fan-out still runs and is verified by log inspection, but the user-visible notification surface in the demo is the in-app inbox (`P-U-08`), not OS-level push.
4	
5	## Consequences
6	
7	- Estimated remaining work is ~37-40 person-days against 60 person-days of remaining capacity (12 days ?? 5 devs) ??? buffer is thin.
8	- "Demo works" means seeded emulator data + happy path + main error states across all 16 prototype screens, not a production Firebase deploy.
9	- Any feature that depends on FCM push *appearing on a device* must have an inbox-based fallback for the demo.
10	- Scaling down is allowed only via explicit ADR amendment, not silently per-page.
11
